_comment_top: MCP server config for exposing the local Ollama instance as a Claude Code tool. Paste the inner mcpServers block into ~/.claude.json (or merge with what's already there). Restart Claude Code for changes to take effect. The local model becomes a callable tool named local_worker.

local_worker._comment: Ollama-MCP shim — exposes the local instance as a tool. Uses npm package @modelcontextprotocol/server-ollama (community-maintained 2026 stable). Adjust the model env to whichever you want as default; the tool can override per-call.

env.MCP_DESCRIPTION: Local Ollama worker on RTX 3060. Use for: high-volume summarisation, log analysis, schema validation, JSON re-formatting. Reserve Cloud (Opus 4.7 / Sonnet) for: code surgery, multi-file pivots, high-stakes reasoning. Free; ~70 t/s on the loaded hot tier.

use_local_for: extracting structured fields from short prose
high-volume batch tasks where a 65% accuracy is acceptable + Haiku is the escalation backstop
use_cloud_for: anything that touches production data flow
anything where wrong output costs more than 30 seconds of review

_install_steps: 1. Open ~/.claude.json (or its equivalent on this machine)
2. Merge the mcpServers block above into the file's mcpServers section
3. Restart Claude Code (exit + re-enter the project)
4. Verify with /mcp list — local_worker should appear
5. From any session, ask: 'Have local_worker summarise the last 50 audit_log entries' to test
_models_available_now: qwen2.5:7b  (loaded, ~5.1 GB VRAM, ~70 t/s — the hot tier)
_models_pull_when_ready: phi4:14b      (~8.5 GB — recommended for medium tier per SPEC §6a)
llama3.3:70b  (~42 GB — heavy tier; pull overnight)
